$comment: otelopus generation manifest. Declares what to generate, from what, to where. A generator reads this + VERSION.lock and produces deterministic output.
description: PLACEHOLDER — this file defines the generation targets but no generator consumes it yet. It exists to break the build if someone tries to run `nuke OtelopusGenerate` without implementing the generator first.

targets.description: Generate C# record types from proto message definitions
targets.generator: TODO: implement proto-to-csharp generator or use protoc + grpc_csharp_plugin
targets.notes: Currently generated via TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> C# codegen (Stages 2-4). This target would replace that chain with a direct proto -> C# path for the wire format types.

targets.description: Generate semantic convention constants from YAML models
targets.notes: Stage 1 of the current pipeline. Already works. This manifest just documents it.

targets.description: Generate domain-specific attribute facade classes from qyl-extensions.json
Generate DuckDB CREATE TABLE statements from proto + semconv promoted columns
targets.generator: TODO: currently generated via TypeSpec -> OpenAPI -> C# codegen (Stage 4). Could be driven directly from proto + promoted-columns.g.sql.

targets.notes: Stage 2. Already works. TypeSpec consumes semconv.g.tsp from target 'semconv-constants'.

targets.description: Generate TypeScript API types from OpenAPI
PLACEHOLDER — validate that generated output matches VERSION.lock checksums
targets.generator: TODO: implement as nuke OtelopusValidate target
targets.notes: This target does not exist yet. It should: (1) verify all VERSION.lock checksums, (2) run nuke Generate, (3) assert zero git diff in .g.* files. If any step fails, the pipeline is non-deterministic.

invariants: All .g.* files are sacred — fix the generator input, never the output.
nuke Generate on a clean checkout with pinned versions must produce zero diff.
Changing any file in otelopus/ requires re-running the full pipeline.
VERSION.lock checksums must match the actual files in otelopus/proto/ and otelopus/semconv/.
The proto files are copies, not submodules. Update by copying from a pinned commit of opentelemetry-proto.
The semconv YAML files are copies, not submodules. Update by copying from a pinned commit of semantic-conventions.
